The '''Red Nebula priest''' resided in the [[Red Nebula temple|temple]] in the [[Red Nebula/Legends|Red Nebula]] which housed [[Great Life Jewels|two crimson jewels]] important to the priest's religion.<ref name="marvel50" />

==Biography==
He believed that the [[Great Life Jewels|jewels]] protected their [[Planet of the Red Nebula|planet]] from the destruction which ravaged all other planets in the [[Red Nebula/Legends|Red Nebula]]. Another [[Red Nebula exiles|group]] believed that the jewels should be destroyed as they were abominations to [[Cosmic Destiny]]. This group was [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]], but returned to the Red Nebula having captured a number of [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] on [[Terminus/Legends|Terminus]] and forced them to [[Attack on the Red Nebula temple|attempt to steal the jewels]] from the [[Red Nebula temple|temple]].<ref name="marvel50" />

Two of the mercenaries they captured were [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and the pair were the first to enter the temple and encounter the priest. To prevent the jewels from being taken, the priest called upon the [[Red Nebula temple guardian|temple guardian]] to safeguard the sacred gems. However, the guardian was distracted by Chewbacca, who was similar in appearance to the guardian except for being much smaller.<ref name="marvel50" />

Using the distraction to his advantage, Solo took the jewels and brought them back to the exiles' spokesman, [[Klysk]]. Klysk and the mercenaries took the jewels from Solo and left the smuggler and his partner stranded on the planet; the exiles then piloted [[Nova Prince|their ship]] into the [[Sun of the Planet of the Red Nebula|sun]], intending to destroy the gems and themselves. However, Solo had hidden the real jewels and given fakes to the exiles, thereby saving the planet from destruction according to the priest's beliefs. In return for saving the jewels, the priest provided a [[Starship/Legends|spacecraft]] to Solo and Chewbacca to return to Terminus.<ref name="marvel50" />

[[Standard year/Legends|Years]] later, [[Domina Tagge/Legends|Domina Tagge]] hired a group of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] to find the rumored crimson gems of the Red Nebula. They found the planet where the jewels were located and [[Mission to the Red Nebula|captured]] both the gems and the priest tending them. When Tagge's [[Mining Explorer]] left with the gems it was quickly confronted and boarded by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] troops, a firefight ensued between the crews and the Imperials captured one of the crimson jewels. In the confusion, the priest, who was dying of wounds he experienced during the fight, released the other crimson jewel. With one jewel exposed without its partner, it unleashed a deadly effect known as the [[Crimson Forever]] on the crew of the Mining Explorer. The effect was reversed when the jewel was reunited with its mate.<ref name="marvel50" />

==Behind the scenes==
This priest appears to be Human, but with pointy ears. [[Leland Chee]], on the [[StarWars.com Message Boards]], stated his theory that "all pointy-eared species are either [[Sephi/Legends|Sephi]] or closely related to Sephi."<ref name="TT">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=1395#16675997|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2007-06-08|time=6:48 AM|quote=For the longest time we've used dozens of pointy-eared characters without a species name for them. The pointed-eared species in the Jedi: Yoda comic were named Sephi. My theory is that all pointy-eared species are either Sephi or closely related to Sephi.}}</ref>

This priest shares many characteristics with [[Darth Vader's secretary]].
